title: Enabling AI to Understand Complex Runtime Behavior for Accurate, Automated Root Cause Analysis — DAC 2026 - Semiwiki
gist: Vendor-authored DAC 2026 promo piece from Undo argues that AI coding agents cannot accurately debug C/C++, SystemC, and EDA verification failures from source code and logs alone, and that Undo's execution-recording technology supplies the missing runtime context; it name-drops AMD verification teams and Nvidia system architects as users without disclosing specifics.

themes.theme: AI needs runtime evidence, not just static logs

insights.claim: Generic LLMs, even those fine-tuned on proprietary engineering data, cannot capture the deep experience-based knowledge verification engineers use to diagnose failures, because they lack runtime context from the live design that only complete execution recordings can provide.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Frames a specific, defensible moat for runtime-recording/time-travel-debug vendors as AI-assisted verification scales, versus generic LLM tooling entering EDA.
insights.quote: Runtime context from the live design is far more valuable than only static training data or generic LLM intelligence. Undo provides that runtime context – and you can't get that data any other way.

insights.claim: Unlike RTL verification, which benefits from waveforms capturing signal activity over time, C/C++ and SystemC verification has no equivalent runtime representation, forcing AI agents to infer behavior from source code and logs alone and reducing root-cause-analysis accuracy.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Identifies a specific gap (no 'waveform equivalent' for software-level verification) that AI-debug tool vendors targeting system-level and HLS verification are trying to fill.
insights.quote: In C/C++ and SystemC, there is no equivalent representation of runtime behavior.

themes.theme: Customer traction and target use cases

insights.claim: Undo markets that verification teams at AMD and system design architects at Nvidia are using its runtime-recording technology, though the article provides no quantified detail and directs interested parties to contact Undo's DAC track chair directly.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: A named-customer claim (AMD, Nvidia) in unverified vendor marketing copy is a lead worth confirming before treating as evidence of adoption at either chipmaker.
insights.quote: Curious about how verification teams at AMD and system design architects at NVIDIA are using Undo's technology?

insights.claim: Undo positions its core value proposition around long regression debug cycles and root-cause analysis across complex verification stacks (testbench, SystemC models, virtual platforms, firmware, RTL), plus enabling EDA R&D and application-engineering teams to reproduce and triage hard-to-reproduce, IP-restricted customer escalations.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Defines the addressable buyer as EDA vendors' own R&D/AE teams and large semiconductor system-verification groups, a narrower but sticky enterprise wedge versus broad developer tooling.
